title: An Accidental Trip to Nana's House
summary: Seeker the wizard meets Isabella, who shows him her dollhouse-cleaning and garden flags. Seeking to explore, Seeker whips up a potion using grass, soft red flowers, water, and a pinch of dirt from Isabella's backyard. After drinking the potion, they are unexpectedly transported in the dark of night to Isabella's Nana's house instead. Assisted by the cat Gorp, who sniffs the air and identifies the scent of other cats and Nana's wild bear, Seeker learns about Nana's house. Isabella shows Seeker around the room, highlighting her blue dollhouse and a cozy block castle housing sleeping bunnies Silona and Syleaf. Relieved to hear that his goldfish Sanana is being fed by an automatic fish feeder back home, Seeker decides to stay the night. He settles into a room in the blue dollhouse and drifts to sleep under a brown cloak as Isabella sings him a sweet, soothing lullaby.

highlightQuote.text: It means that I really need to clean my dollhouses a lot. That's what I need. That's what my flag means.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Isabella.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hello, Isabella.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do you like this flag?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my goodness, can you tell me about it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I made it with sparkly paper, glue, popsicle sticks, and stickers, a pipe cleaner, and paper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, it's a beautiful flag. Now, a flag usually means something. You wave it to show something that you care about. So what does your flag mean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It means that I really need to clean my dollhouses a lot. That's what I need. That's what my flag means.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's a dollhouse cleaning flag. Well, I would proudly fly a flag like that above my castle.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And there's a flower one that means take good care of your garden, Nana.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. I take good care of my garden too, so I definitely would like to have a flag like that too.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, what's, what do you grow in your garden?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well, you know, I need all sorts of different plants for potion ingredients. So I grow a lot of things that not everyone grows, you know? I grow lizard berries and stink flowers... and some yucca grass.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stink flowers? I don't like stink flowers. I like the regular kind.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I have some of those too, to help balance out the stink flowers. But they're very useful for potions.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Seeker, when you come to my house, you might like my garden.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what have you got there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I just got fuzzy plants, and lots of big red flowers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That does sound interesting. I'd like to learn about it. Well, should we make a 'going to Isabella's backyard' potion?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What ingredients do you need for that?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, you'll have to tell me more about your backyard for me to find out. Now, for instance, do you have any grass in your backyard?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Uh-huh.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, okay. So, we'll put some grass in, and Seeker started to get a potion ready. And what else have you got back there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A garden with flowers, soft plants, red flowers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, red... okay, okay. And Seeker got together a bunch of different plant ingredients. And is there anything else that's not a plant in your backyard?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hmm, swing set, climbing structure, playhouse, water table.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. Oh, water. Yeah, we'd better add some water to the potion.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Water table.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, I don't think I can add a swing set or a playhouse to the potion, unless we had a very small one that we could grind up.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't think so.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No. What else should we add? Do you have any gravel or rocks or anything?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Or any dirt?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. Only a tiny bit, like maybe...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A pinch, we call that. All right, let's add a pinch of dirt. And then we'll shake it up. Shake, shake, shake, shake, shake. Give it a sniff. Hm, it smells like the outdoors.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, Isabella, would you like to take a sip?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and Seeker took a sip. Okay, say the rhyme, please, Isabella.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Slime-ka-by, slime-ka-by, byme.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Slime-ka-by, slime-ka-by, byme. And then a third time.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Slime-ka-by, slime-ka-by, byme.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then, there appeared with a gentle pop, on the stone patio behind Isabella's house. But, they couldn't really see much of anything. You know why?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Why?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Because it was dark out. It was late at night at Isabella's house.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Seeker?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Was today a sleepover?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ah, I think it turned out to be... oh, potion doesn't know which house, so, I think it took us... let me sniff the air. Um, I brought my cat crew, so, the cat crew... so, one of my cats, so I'm going to say, volunteer one of the cats, and so they can sniff the air.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, sniff the air, said Seeker, that's interesting. And there's a, 'Meow'.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Are you Gorp?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Gorp said, 'Meow, that's me'.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can you sniff the air, see if it smells like my house or Nana's house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Sniff, sniff, sniff, sniff. Hmm. Smells a little bit like chicken. Somebody's been eating chicken. Do you have any chicken?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, no.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no. Which house does it smell like?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Or kibble? Have you got any kibble?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Which house does it smell like?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: This house, we are definitely near a house, said Gorp. And it smells like...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nana's or my house?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It smells like Isabella's house because I can smell Silver Cat and Finn Cat in there. And I think maybe they had chicken for dinner recently. Or maybe there's some in the trash can. Or maybe it's the neighbor, I don't know. But we're...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or maybe it's at the neighbor's. But I'm definitely at my Nana's. Do you smell any Garfields? Because I do. I smell the smell of Garfield's.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Gorp said, 'Now that you mention it...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I smell the smell of Garfield's. Do you?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I think, I do smell another cat. Is Garfield a cat?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Garfield's a cat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, wait a minute, I smell something funny. It's an animal. Smells like a... one I don't normally interact with.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But it's a cat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, it's not a cat.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Who is it then? Is it a bear that lives in the woods? Is it Nana's bear?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I think it might be! It smells like a creature that eats lots of nuts and berries and is very big, and sleeps all winter.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So it's, so I think it's my Nana's. So, Seeker, it took the potion to my Nana's.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: To my Nana's.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's unusual. You know, normally the potions go just where we expect them to, or we have no idea where we're going to go. And we tried to make an Isabella's backyard potion, but we accidentally made a Nana's house potion. But Nana's house seems nice too. Can you show me around, please? said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Of course.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That is the blue dollhouse, the big three, I set up the bunny house, grocery store, hardware store, the bakery, my dresser, my overnight diapers, the chair, the bookshelf, the fan, the doors, pictures, my bed.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. That is very interesting. And what about that little castle over there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What little castle?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It appears to be a structure made out of blocks.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's the bunny house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The bunny house. Tell me about that one.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, do you hear the bunnies sleeping?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I do, said Seeker. That looks like a very cozy house for them to sleep in.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They have two babies. Two.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what are their names?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Silona and Syleaf.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Silona and Syleaf?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, those are beautiful bunny names.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They tend to do a lot of hopping at bedtime.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, do you want to spend most time at Nana's and most time at your castle? Cuz, there's going to be somebody who watches your house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Somebody who watches my house? What do you mean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, the cat sitters, if you have any pets.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do you have a pet dog or a pet cat, or, or a pet bird or a pet goldfish?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hmm. Let's decide. What kind of pet does Seeker have back at his castle?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, a goldfish.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, 'Yes, I do have a goldfish back home, so I can't stay away too long cuz I need to feed him. What's Seeker's goldfish's name?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But there's, but there's a fish feeder. Uh, and his goldfish's name's Sanana.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Sanana, uh-huh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, Sanana might get hungry.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, but you're not going to be the one to feed him.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I am not?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The fish feeder's going to feed him.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, but I'm all the way here at Nana's house, and I've got to get in touch with this fish feeder. How are we going to make sure that the fish feeder comes by and gives the fish the right kind of food, the right amount, and everything?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and talks to the fish. The fish... Sanana really likes to be talked to a lot, you know.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Uh, well, she knows how much cuz she's been to your house before.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's good. Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Remember when you were away at your papa's house?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yeah. I had a fish feeder then. That's true.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, that's good. I can relax knowing that my fish will be taken care of. And so maybe I could stay overnight here with you at Nana's house. What do you think, Seeker?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That would be good.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, well, where can I sleep?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: In with one in my room.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thanks.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And downstairs is too noisy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's too noisy. Why is it so noisy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cuz there's just a lot of people down there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, like who?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nana, Ripper Rick, Garfield...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Smoky, Tommy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Smoky and Tommy, wow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hmm. Do you think the bunnies would let me sleep in their castle, or should I try one of the dollhouses?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can try one of the dollhouses.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Do you want to live in the blue dollhouse?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I do. That sounds very nice. That's the big one, right? Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And is there a bedroom there that I could use?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, I can show you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, which room?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You go...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Go in this room, Seeker, it has a nice brown cloak.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, that sounds lovely. Thank you. And since I've never slept here before, would you mind just doing me a favor, Isabella, said Seeker. Would you please just tell me a bedtime story? Help me go to sleep, please.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Not right now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Maybe you could sing me a song?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Goodnight Seeker. Wake up in the morning, sweet dreams. Say goodnight. Sleep tight. Oh Seeker, how life feel. Oh no. New places are hard. When you go to sleep, it feels more better, when you tell me why. Oh Seeker. I know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And there was a gentle 'zzz' coming from Seeker's bedroom, because the beautiful lullaby that Isabella sang for him put him to sleep. And that's the end of Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm taking...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here, here is your phone.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you.

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Gorp
characters.description: A cat traveler who journeys to Cat World and discovers his role in the story.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: Nana
characters.description: The dignified mole matriarch and grandmother to Hattie, Pistis, and Isabella, this pillar of watchful wisdom resides at the end of the train line, tackling life’s problems "one at a time." From engineering mouse-scale Lego structures to the meticulous restoration of dollhouses using patient glue and tiny pencils, her steady paws bridge the gap between architectural feats and delicate artistry. Whether discerning potential as a trolley passenger or welcoming weary travelers into her sanctuary, her quiet authority is so legendary that she graces the cover of Hattie’s book in a rare, peaceful slumber. Even in rest, she remains the heart of their world—a steady warmth anchoring every journey’s end, her sharp eyes softening for Izzy’s mushroom-textured crafts.

characters.name: Silver Cat
characters.description: A cat who receives a medium-sized, sparkly silver cardboard hat from Hattie.

characters.name: Finn Cat
characters.description: A character featured in a coloring picture with Worry-Worry Whale.

characters.name: Garfield
characters.description: Minnie and Mickey’s intrepid kitten is a seasoned morning commuter, navigating the school run to reach Seeker’s sanctuary for heavy napping and premium kibble. A social fixture known for sharing twilight dinners with Ginger or waiting expectantly at Nana’s for a warm welcome, they occasionally trade their public life for the quiet solitude of a closet, sequestering themselves away with a treasured hoard of toys.

characters.name: Nana's Bear
characters.description: A large woodland bear that eats nuts and berries near Nana's house.

characters.name: Silona
characters.description: A baby bunny living in a cozy block house at Nana's.

characters.name: Syleaf
characters.description: Another baby bunny living in the block house who enjoys hopping around at bedtime.

characters.name: Sanana
characters.description: Seeker's pet goldfish who lives back at the castle and loves to be talked to.

characters.name: Ripper Rick
characters.description: A family member downstairs at Nana's house whose presence makes the lower floor quite noisy.

characters.name: Smoky
characters.description: A resident or pet hanging out in the noisy downstairs of Nana's house.

characters.name: Tommy
characters.description: Another person or pet located in the noisy downstairs area of Nana's house.

places.name: Seeker's Castle
places.description: Behind an unassuming facade and a solitary doorbell lies Seeker’s home, a sprawling magical fortress that serves as a vital sanctuary for weary legends. The interior expands into a labyrinthine refuge where an expansive library of mystical books towers beside a potion closet that acts as the true gateway to adventure. This fortress is a hub for calls to action and well-earned rest, where Wobbly Whiskers waits to greet the Seeker and Buddy the Mouse—though the warmth of their homecoming is inevitably punctuated by his latest sticky mess upon the rug.

places.name: Isabella's Backyard
places.description: The intended destination of Seeker's travel potion, containing soft red flowers, grass, and a play area.

places.name: Nana's house
places.description: At the final stop of the long train journey stands a meticulously crafted dollhouse, a quiet sanctuary where tiny, sharpened pencils await the scholarly paws of resident mice.

places.name: The Blue Dollhouse
places.description: A large three-story dollhouse at Nana's house where Seeker is invited to spend the night in a cozy bedroom with a brown cloak.

places.name: The Bunny House
places.description: A small block structure at Nana's house where a family of bunnies, including babies Silona and Syleaf, sleep.
